name: Copenhagen Flan
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Cold water to mix as necessary
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare a moderately hot oven (400F, Gas Mark 6).
Place an 8-inch fluted flan ring on a baking sheet.
2. In a bowl, combine plain flour and salt. Add fats cut into small pieces and rub in with fingertips until the mixture resembles fine breadcrumbs. Gradually add about 1 1/2 tbsp cold water and mix with a fork to form a firm dough. Turn out onto a floured board and lightly knead. Roll out pastry to a circle, about 1-1/2 inches larger all around than the flan ring. Place pastry on rolling pin and lift into flan rings. Gently ease pastry into flan ring and press into flutes. Roll off surplus pastry with rolling pin across top of flan case. Press pastry into flutes again with fingers.
3. Spread jam in base of flan case.
4. Cream 2 oz margarine and the castor sugar together until light. Beat egg and add gradually, beating well after each addition. Fold in self-raising flour. Place 4 tablespoons of the reserved milk (or evaporated milk) in a small saucepan; set aside for topping. Stir remainder into creamed mixture with a few drops of almond essence.
5. Pour mixture into flan case. Bake in the center of oven for 20 to 25 minutes, remove flan ring.
6. Meanwhile, make topping. Chop almonds and place on a baking sheet in the bottom of oven for about 10 minutes until browned.
7. Add butter and brown sugar to reserved milk in saucepan; stir over moderate heat until sugar has melted. Bring to boil and cook, stirring for 1 minute. Stir in browned almonds.
8. Spread topping over flan and return flan to oven. Cook for a further 10 minutes. Serve warm or cold.
